topic: What happens in your brain during deep sleep
narration: Your brain literally washes itself every night while you sleep. Here's how it works. During deep sleep, your brain cells actually shrink. They get smaller, up to sixty percent smaller. This opens up these tiny spaces between them. And then something incredible happens. Cerebrospinal fluid rushes through like a power washer, flushing out all the toxic waste that built up during the day. We're talking proteins linked to Alzheimer's, metabolic junk, cellular debris. This glymphatic system only kicks into high gear when you're in deep sleep. Skip the deep sleep? Your brain doesn't get its wash cycle. So when someone says they'll sleep when they're dead, remind them their brain is literally cleaning itself to prevent exactly that.

caption:
Your brain has a self-cleaning system that ONLY works during deep sleep 🧠✨

The glymphatic system is one of the most fascinating discoveries in neuroscience. When you enter deep sleep, your neurons actually shrink to create space for cerebrospinal fluid to flush out toxic proteins (including those linked to Alzheimer's and dementia).

This is why consistently poor sleep is associated with cognitive decline and neurodegenerative diseases. Your brain literally needs those deep sleep cycles to take out the trash.

Prioritizing 7-9 hours of quality sleep isn't lazy—it's essential brain maintenance. What's your biggest barrier to good sleep? Let me know below! 👇
